-------------------------------- MODULE Sets_IsBijectionInverse --------------------------------
EXTENDS Integers, NaturalsInduction, TLAPS
  \** NB: Module NaturalsInduction comes from the TLAPS library, usually
  \** installed in /usr/local/lib/tlaps. Make sure this is in your Toolbox
  \** search path, see Preferences/TLA+ Preferences.

IsBijection(f, S, T) == /\ f \in [S -> T]
                        /\ \A x, y \in S : (x # y) => (f[x] # f[y])
                        /\ \A y \in T : \E x \in S : f[x] = y


IsFiniteSet(S) == \E n \in Nat : \E f : IsBijection(f, 1..n, S)

(****************************************************************************)
(* Finite sets and cardinality are defined in the TLA+ standard module      *)
(* FiniteSets, but this is not yet natively supported by TLAPS. For the     *)
(* time being, we use the following axiom for defining set cardinality.     *)
(****************************************************************************)
\* Cardinality(S) == CHOOSE n : (n \in Nat) /\ \E f : IsBijection(f, 1..n, S)

CONSTANT Cardinality(_)
AXIOM CardinalityAxiom ==
         \A S : IsFiniteSet(S) =>
           \A n : (n = Cardinality(S)) <=>
                    (n \in Nat) /\ \E f : IsBijection(f, 1..n, S)
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

THEOREM CardinalityInNat == \A S : IsFiniteSet(S) => Cardinality(S) \in Nat
  PROOF OMITTED

------------------------------------------------------------------

THEOREM CardinalityZero ==
           /\ IsFiniteSet({})
           /\ Cardinality({}) = 0
           /\ \A S : IsFiniteSet(S) /\ (Cardinality(S)=0) => (S = {})
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM CardinalityPlusOne ==
    ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S),
           NEW x, x \notin S
    PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(S \cup {x})
           /\ Cardinality(S \cup {x}) = Cardinality(S) + 1
  PROOF OMITTED

------------------------------------------------------------------

THEOREM CardinalityOne == \A m : /\ IsFiniteSet({m})
                                 /\ Cardinality({m}) = 1
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM CardinalityTwo == \A m, p : m # p => 
                              /\ IsFiniteSet({m,p})
                              /\ Cardinality({m,p}) = 2
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM IntervalCardinality ==  
  ASSUME NEW a \in Nat, NEW b \in Nat 
  PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(a..b)
         /\ Cardinality(a..b) = IF a > b THEN 0 ELSE b-a+1
  PROOF OMITTED

------------------------------------------------------------------

THEOREM CardinalityOneConverse ==
   ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S), Cardinality(S) = 1
   PROVE  \E m : S = {m}
  PROOF OMITTED

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

THEOREM IsBijectionInverse ==
  ASSUME NEW f, NEW S, NEW T, 
         IsBijection(f, S, T) 
  PROVE  \E g : IsBijection(g, T, S)
PROOF OBVIOUS

=============================================================================

item.text: The inverse is definable via `CHOOSE`; I’m patching a structured proof around that witness.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "apply_patch <<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: Sets_IsBijectionInverse.tla
@@
 THEOREM IsBijectionInverse ==
   ASSUME NEW f, NEW S, NEW T, 
          IsBijection(f, S, T) 
   PROVE  \\E g : IsBijection(g, T, S)
-PROOF OBVIOUS
+PROOF
+  DEFINE g == [t \\in T |-> CHOOSE s \\in S : f[s] = t]
+  <1>1. \\A t \\in T : /\\ g[t] \\in S
+                        /\\ f[g[t]] = t
+    PROOF
+      <2>1. TAKE t \\in T
+      <2>2. \\E s \\in S : f[s] = t
+        BY DEF IsBijection
+      <2>3. /\\ (CHOOSE s \\in S : f[s] = t) \\in S
+             /\\ f[(CHOOSE s \\in S : f[s] = t)] = t
+        BY <2>2
+      <2>4. QED
+        BY <2>3 DEF g
+    QED
+  <1>2. g \\in [T -> S]
+    BY <1>1 DEF g
+  <1>3. \\A x, y \\in T : (x # y) => (g[x] # g[y])
+    PROOF
+      <2>1. TAKE x \\in T, y \\in T
+      <2>2. ASSUME x # y
+             PROVE  g[x] # g[y]
+        BY <1>1
+    QED
+  <1>4. \\A y \\in S : \\E x \\in T : g[x] = y
+    PROOF
+      <2>1. TAKE y \\in S
+      <2>2. f[y] \\in T
+        BY DEF IsBijection
+      <2>3. /\\ g[f[y]] \\in S
+             /\\ f[g[f[y]]] = f[y]
+        BY <1>1, <2>2
+      <2>4. g[f[y]] = y
+        BY <2>3 DEF IsBijection
+      <2>5. QED
+        BY <2>2, <2>4
+    QED
+  <1>5. IsBijection(g, T, S)
+    BY <1>2, <1>3, <1>4 DEF IsBijection
+  <1>6. QED
+    BY <1>5
 
 =============================================================================
*** End Patch
PATCH
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps Sets_IsBijectionInverse.tla"

THEOREM WFMin ==
         ASSUME NEW R, NEW S, 
                IsWellFoundedOn(R, S),
                NEW T, T \subseteq S, T # {}
         PROVE  \E x \in T : \A y \in T : ~ (<<y, x>> \in R)
<1> SUFFICES ASSUME \A x \in T : \E y \in T : <<y, x>> \in R
             PROVE  FALSE
  BY Zenon
<1> DEFINE f0 == CHOOSE x \in T : TRUE
           Def(v, n) == CHOOSE x \in T : <<x, v>> \in R
           f[n \in Nat] == IF n = 0 THEN f0 ELSE Def(f[n-1], n)
<1>1. NatInductiveDefConclusion(f, f0, Def)
  <2>1. NatInductiveDefHypothesis(f, f0, Def)
    BY DEF NatInductiveDefHypothesis
  <2>. QED
    BY <2>1, NatInductiveDef   
<1>2. f \in [Nat -> T]
  <2>1. f0 \in T
    OBVIOUS
  <2>2. \A v \in T, n \in Nat \ {0} : Def(v, n) \in T
    OBVIOUS
  <2>. QED
    BY <1>1, <2>1, <2>2, NatInductiveDefType, Isa
<1>3. ASSUME NEW n \in Nat 
      PROVE  <<f[n+1], f[n]>> \in R 
  (* FIXME: SMT backend raises exception "Index past end of list" 
  BY <1>1, <1>2 DEF NatInductiveDefConclusion
  *)
  <2>. /\ n+1 \in Nat
       /\ n+1 # 0
       /\ (n+1)-1 = n
    OBVIOUS
  <2>. QED
    BY <1>1, <1>2, Zenon DEF NatInductiveDefConclusion
<1>. QED
  BY <1>2, <1>3, Zenon DEF IsWellFoundedOn


THEOREM MinWF ==
         ASSUME NEW R, NEW S,
                \A T \in SUBSET S : T # {} => \E x \in T : \A y \in T : ~ (<<y, x>> \in R)

PROVE  IsWellFoundedOn(RR,S)
<1>1. SUFFICES ASSUME NEW f \in [Nat -> S],
                      \A n \in Nat : <<f[n+1], f[n]>> \in RR
               PROVE  FALSE
  BY DEF IsWellFoundedOn
<1>2. \A n \in Nat : <<f[n+1], f[n]>> \in RR \cap (S \X S)
  BY <1>1
<1>. QED  BY <1>2 DEF IsWellFoundedOn

(***************************************************************************)
(* If we think of R as a less-than relation on S, then the following is    *)
(* the set of elements of S that are less than x.                          *)
(***************************************************************************)
SetLessThan(x, R, S) ==  {y \in S : <<y, x>> \in R}

(***************************************************************************)
(* If we think of R as a less-than relation on S, then R is well-founded   *)
(* iff every non-empty subset of S has a minimal element.                  *)
(***************************************************************************)

THEOREM WFMin ==
         ASSUME NEW R, NEW S, 
                IsWellFoundedOn(R, S),
                NEW T, T \subseteq S, T # {}
         PROVE  \E x \in T : \A y \in T : ~ (<<y, x>> \in R)
<1> SUFFICES ASSUME \A x \in T : \E y \in T : <<y, x>> \in R
(* Given sequence seq, SeqToBag(seq) is a Bag                              *)
(***************************************************************************)

THEOREM Bags_IsABagSeqToBag ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW seq \in Seq(S)
  PROVE  IsABag(SeqToBag(seq))
<1>. DEFINE occs(x) == { i \in DOMAIN seq : seq[i] = x }
<1>1. ASSUME NEW x \in Range(seq)
      PROVE  Cardinality(occs(x)) \in Nat \ {0}
  <2>1. /\ IsFiniteSet(occs(x))
        /\ occs(x) # {}
    BY SeqDef, FS_Interval, FS_Subset DEF Range
  <2>. QED  BY <2>1, FS_CardinalityType, FS_EmptySet, Zenon
<1>. QED  BY <1>1 DEF IsABag, SeqToBag

=============================================================================

(* Contributed by Damien Doligez *)

-------------- MODULE Cantor1 -----------------
THEOREM cantor ==
  \A S :
    \A f \in [S -> SUBSET S] :
      \E A \in SUBSET S :
        \A x \in S :
          f [x] # A
PROOF
  <1>1. TAKE S
  <1>2. TAKE f \in [S -> SUBSET S]
  <1>3. DEFINE T == { z \in S : z \notin f[z] }
  <1>4. WITNESS T \in SUBSET S
  <1>5. TAKE x \in S
  <1>6. QED BY x \in T \/ x \notin T
===============================================
\* Then just prove that for each n \in 1..N, where P(n) is fulfilled, the requirement P(n-1) 
\* is fulfilled, i.e. <>cnt=(n-1) 
\* This then proves, if cnt can be in 0..N that P(0) will be reached, i.e. <>cnt=0; which
\*  equals Termination 
THEOREM WillReachZero == Spec => Termination    
PROOF
\* The liveness proof uses an intermediate predicate for downward induction
\* Note that P(0) just expresses the assertion of the theorem.
<1> DEFINE P(m) == Spec => <>(cnt = m)  
<1> HIDE DEF P    
<1>2. P(0)  
    <2>1. P(N)  
\* Start condition - this is actually obvious. We show that initialization fulfills the first goal  
        BY PTL DEF P, Spec, Init
    <2>2. \A n \in 1..N : P(n) => P(n-1)  
\* prove: for all intermediate goals, the next goal will be reached
        <3>1. SUFFICES
                ASSUME NEW n \in 1 .. N

/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '136,146p' ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps/WellFoundedInduction_proofs.tla && sed -n '184,194p' ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps/WellFoundedInduction_proofs.tla"

(***************************************************************************)
LEMMA WellFoundedIsIrreflexive ==
        ASSUME NEW R, NEW S, NEW x \in S,
               IsWellFoundedOn(R, S)
        PROVE  <<x, x>> \notin R
<1>1. \E z \in {x} : \A y \in {x} : <<y,z>> \notin R
  BY WFMin, IsaM("blast")
<1>2. QED
  BY <1>1


          PROVE  \A x \in S : P(x)
<1> DEFINE T == {x \in S : ~P(x)}
<1>1. SUFFICES ASSUME T # {}
               PROVE  FALSE
  OBVIOUS
<1>2. PICK x \in T : \A y \in T : ~ (<<y, x>> \in R)
  BY <1>1, WFMin, Zenon
<1>3. QED
  BY <1>2 DEF SetLessThan

(***************************************************************************)

item.text: The parser issue is proof-step shape, not logic; I’m normalizing QED steps to library style.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "apply_patch <<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: Sets_IsBijectionInverse.tla
@@
-      <2>4. QED
+      <2> QED
@@
-      <2>5. QED
+      <2> QED
@@
-  <1>6. QED
+  <1> QED
*** End Patch
PATCH
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps Sets_IsBijectionInverse.tla"

/bin/zsh -lc "apply_patch <<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: Sets_IsBijectionInverse.tla
@@
-      <2>4. QED
+      <2> QED
@@
-      <2>5. QED
+      <2> QED
@@
-  <1>6. QED
+  <1> QED
*** End Patch
PATCH
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps Sets_IsBijectionInverse.tla"

item.text: I found the culprit: nested steps don’t use a `PROOF` wrapper. Patching that now.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "apply_patch <<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: Sets_IsBijectionInverse.tla
@@
   <1>1. \\A t \\in T : /\\ g[t] \\in S
                         /\\ f[g[t]] = t
-    PROOF
       <2>1. TAKE t \\in T
       <2>2. \\E s \\in S : f[s] = t
         BY DEF IsBijection
@@
       <2> QED
         BY <2>3 DEF g
-    QED
   <1>2. g \\in [T -> S]
@@
   <1>3. \\A x, y \\in T : (x # y) => (g[x] # g[y])
-    PROOF
       <2>1. TAKE x \\in T, y \\in T
       <2>2. ASSUME x # y
              PROVE  g[x] # g[y]
         BY <1>1
-    QED
   <1>4. \\A y \\in S : \\E x \\in T : g[x] = y
-    PROOF
       <2>1. TAKE y \\in S
       <2>2. f[y] \\in T
         BY DEF IsBijection
@@
       <2> QED
         BY <2>2, <2>4
-    QED
*** End Patch
PATCH
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps Sets_IsBijectionInverse.tla"

item.text: For this theorem form, TLAPS accepts a step proof directly after `PROVE`; I’m switching to that style.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "apply_patch <<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: Sets_IsBijectionInverse.tla
@@
-  PROVE  \\E g : IsBijection(g, T, S)
-PROOF
-  <1>0. DEFINE g == [t \\in T |-> CHOOSE s \\in S : f[s] = t]
+  PROVE  \\E g : IsBijection(g, T, S)
+<1>. DEFINE g == [t \\in T |-> CHOOSE s \\in S : f[s] = t]
*** End Patch
PATCH
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps Sets_IsBijectionInverse.tla"

/\ \A e \in Edges(Nodes) :$
                            \E m, n \in Nodes : e = {m, n}$
BY IsaM("force") DEF Edges$
$
-------------------------------------------------------$
LEMMA EdgesFinite == $
  ASSUME NEW Nodes, IsFiniteSet(Nodes)$
  PROVE  IsFiniteSet(Edges(Nodes))$
<1>1. IsFiniteSet(Nodes \X Nodes)$
  BY FS_Product$
<1>2. [m \in Nodes \X Nodes |-> {m[1], m[2]}] \in Surjection(Nodes \X Nodes, Edges(Nodes))$
  BY Fun_IsSurj, Zenon DEF Edges$
<1>. QED  BY <1>1, <1>2, FS_Surjection$
$
NonLoopEdges(Nodes) == {e \in Edges(Nodes) : Cardinality(e) = 2}$
SimpleGraphs(Nodes) == SUBSET NonLoopEdges(Nodes)$
Degree(n, G) == Cardinality ({e \in G : n \in e})$
$
  PROOF OMITTED$
$
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------$
$
THEOREM IsBijectionInverse ==$
  ASSUME NEW f, NEW S, NEW T, $
         IsBijection(f, S, T) $
  PROVE  \E g : IsBijection(g, T, S)$
<1>. DEFINE g == [t \in T |-> CHOOSE s \in S : f[s] = t]$
  <1>1. \A t \in T : /\ g[t] \in S$
                        /\ f[g[t]] = t$
      <2>1. TAKE t \in T$
      <2>2. \E s \in S : f[s] = t$

item.text: Instead of a local proof definition, I’m using a witness expression directly to avoid parser ambiguity.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('Sets_IsBijectionInverse.tla')
s=p.read_text()
start=s.index('THEOREM IsBijectionInverse ==')
end=s.index('\\n=============================================================================', start)
new='''THEOREM IsBijectionInverse ==\\n  ASSUME NEW f, NEW S, NEW T, \\n         IsBijection(f, S, T) \\n  PROVE  \\\\E g : IsBijection(g, T, S)\\nPROOF\\n  <1>1. WITNESS [t \\\\in T |-> CHOOSE s \\\\in S : f[s] = t]\\n  <1>2. \\\\A t \\\\in T : /\\\\ [t \\\\in T |-> CHOOSE s \\\\in S : f[s] = t][t] \\\\in S\\n                        /\\\\ f[[t \\\\in T |-> CHOOSE s \\\\in S : f[s] = t][t]] = t\\n      <2>1. TAKE t \\\\in T\\n      <2>2. \\\\E s \\\\in S : f[s] = t\\n        BY DEF IsBijection\\n      <2>3. /\\\\ (CHOOSE s \\\\in S : f[s] = t) \\\\in S\\n             /\\\\ f[(CHOOSE s \\\\in S : f[s] = t)] = t\\n        BY <2>2\\n      <2> QED\\n        BY <2>3\\n  <1>3. [t \\\\in T |-> CHOOSE s \\\\in S : f[s] = t] \\\\in [T -> S]\\n    BY <1>2\\n  <1>4. \\\\A x, y \\\\in T : (x # y) => ([t \\\\in T |-> CHOOSE s \\\\in S : f[s] = t][x] # [t \\\\in T |-> CHOOSE s \\\\in S : f[s] = t][y])\\n      <2>1. TAKE x \\\\in T, y \\\\in T\\n      <2>2. ASSUME x # y\\n             PROVE  [t \\\\in T |-> CHOOSE s \\\\in S : f[s] = t][x] # [t \\\\in T |-> CHOOSE s \\\\in S : f[s] = t][y]\\n        BY <1>2\\n  <1>5. \\\\A y \\\\in S : \\\\E x \\\\in T : [t \\\\in T |-> CHOOSE s \\\\in S : f[s] = t][x] = y\\n      <2>1. TAKE y \\\\in S\\n      <2>2. f[y] \\\\in T\\n        BY DEF IsBijection\\n      <2>3. /\\\\ [t \\\\in T |-> CHOOSE s \\\\in S : f[s] = t][f[y]] \\\\in S\\n             /\\\\ f[[t \\\\in T |-> CHOOSE s \\\\in S : f[s] = t][f[y]]] = f[y]\\n        BY <1>2, <2>2\\n      <2>4. [t \\\\in T |-> CHOOSE s \\\\in S : f[s] = t][f[y]] = y\\n        BY <2>3 DEF IsBijection\\n      <2> QED\\n        BY <2>2, <2>4\\n  <1>6. IsBijection([t \\\\in T |-> CHOOSE s \\\\in S : f[s] = t], T, S)\\n    BY <1>3, <1>4, <1>5 DEF IsBijection\\n  <1> QED\\n    BY <1>6\\n'''
p.write_text(s[:start]+new+s[end:])
PY
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps Sets_IsBijectionInverse.tla"

WITNESS [t \\\\in T |-> CHOOSE s \\\\in S : f[s] = t]\\n  <1>2. \\\\A t \\\\in T : /\\\\ [t \\\\in T |-> CHOOSE s \\\\in S : f[s] = t][t] \\\\in S\\n                        /\\\\ f[[t \\\\in T |-> CHOOSE s \\\\in S : f[s] = t][t]] = t\\n      <2>1. TAKE t \\\\in T\\n      <2>2. \\\\E s \\\\in S : f[s] = t\\n        BY DEF IsBijection\\n      <2>3. /\\\\ (CHOOSE s \\\\in S : f[s] = t) \\\\in S\\n             /\\\\ f[(CHOOSE s \\\\in S : f[s] = t)] = t\\n        BY <2>2\\n      <2> QED\\n        BY <2>3\\n  <1>3. [t \\\\in T |-> CHOOSE s \\\\in S : f[s] = t] \\\\in [T -> S]\\n    BY <1>2\\n  <1>4. \\\\A x, y \\\\in T : (x # y) => ([t \\\\in T |-> CHOOSE s \\\\in S : f[s] = t][x] # [t \\\\in T |-> CHOOSE s \\\\in S : f[s] = t][y])\\n      <2>1. TAKE x \\\\in T, y \\\\in T\\n      <2>2. ASSUME x # y\\n             PROVE  [t \\\\in T |-> CHOOSE s \\\\in S : f[s] = t][x] # [t \\\\in T |-> CHOOSE s \\\\in S : f[s] = t][y]\\n        BY <1>2\\n  <1>5. \\\\A y \\\\in S : \\\\E x \\\\in T : [t \\\\in T |-> CHOOSE s \\\\in S : f[s] = t][x] = y\\n      <2>1. [t \\\\in T |-> CHOOSE s \\\\in S : f[s] = t][f[y]] = y\\n        BY <2>3 DEF IsBijection\\n      <2> QED\\n        BY <2>2, <2>4\\n  <1>6. IsBijection([t \\\\in T |-> CHOOSE s \\\\in S : f[s] = t], T, S)\\n    BY <1>3, <1>4, <1>5 DEF IsBijection\\n  <1> QED\\n    BY <1>6\\n'''

item.text: `WITNESS` also closes the existential goal, so I’m proving the witness body first and using it only at QED.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('Sets_IsBijectionInverse.tla')
s=p.read_text()
start=s.index('THEOREM IsBijectionInverse ==')
end=s.index('\\n=============================================================================', start)
new='''THEOREM IsBijectionInverse ==\\n  ASSUME NEW f, NEW S, NEW T, \\n         IsBijection(f, S, T) \\n  PROVE  \\\\E g : IsBijection(g, T, S)\\n<1>. DEFINE g == [t \\\\in T |-> CHOOSE s \\\\in S : f[s] = t]\\n<1>1. \\\\A t \\\\in T : /\\\\ g[t] \\\\in S\\n                      /\\\\ f[g[t]] = t\\n  <2>1. TAKE t \\\\in T\\n  <2>2. \\\\E s \\\\in S : f[s] = t\\n    BY DEF IsBijection\\n  <2>3. /\\\\ (CHOOSE s \\\\in S : f[s] = t) \\\\in S\\n         /\\\\ f[(CHOOSE s \\\\in S : f[s] = t)] = t\\n    BY <2>2\\n  <2>. QED\\n    BY <2>3 DEF g\\n<1>2. g \\\\in [T -> S]\\n  BY <1>1 DEF g\\n<1>3. \\\\A x, y \\\\in T : (x # y) => (g[x] # g[y])\\n  <2>1. TAKE x \\\\in T, y \\\\in T\\n  <2>2. ASSUME x # y\\n         PROVE  g[x] # g[y]\\n    BY <1>1\\n<1>4. \\\\A y \\\\in S : \\\\E x \\\\in T : g[x] = y\\n  <2>1. TAKE y \\\\in S\\n  <2>2. f[y] \\\\in T\\n    BY DEF IsBijection\\n  <2>3. /\\\\ g[f[y]] \\\\in S\\n         /\\\\ f[g[f[y]]] = f[y]\\n    BY <1>1, <2>2\\n  <2>4. g[f[y]] = y\\n    BY <2>3 DEF IsBijection\\n  <2>. QED\\n    BY <2>2, <2>4\\n<1>5. IsBijection(g, T, S)\\n  BY <1>2, <1>3, <1>4 DEF IsBijection\\n<1>. QED\\n  BY <1>5\\n'''
p.write_text(s[:start]+new+s[end:])
PY
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps Sets_IsBijectionInverse.tla"

/bin/zsh -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('Sets_IsBijectionInverse.tla')
s=p.read_text()
start=s.index('THEOREM IsBijectionInverse ==')
end=s.index('\\n=============================================================================', start)
new='''THEOREM IsBijectionInverse ==\\n  ASSUME NEW f, NEW S, NEW T, \\n         IsBijection(f, S, T) \\n  PROVE  \\\\E g : IsBijection(g, T, S)\\n<1>. DEFINE g == [t \\\\in T |-> CHOOSE s \\\\in S : f[s] = t]\\n<1>1. \\\\A t \\\\in T : /\\\\ g[t] \\\\in S\\n                      /\\\\ f[g[t]] = t\\n  <2>1. TAKE t \\\\in T\\n  <2>2. \\\\E s \\\\in S : f[s] = t\\n    BY DEF IsBijection\\n  <2>3. /\\\\ (CHOOSE s \\\\in S : f[s] = t) \\\\in S\\n         /\\\\ f[(CHOOSE s \\\\in S : f[s] = t)] = t\\n    BY <2>2\\n  <2>. QED\\n    BY <2>3 DEF g\\n<1>2. g \\\\in [T -> S]\\n  BY <1>1 DEF g\\n<1>3. \\\\A x, y \\\\in T : (x # y) => (g[x] # g[y])\\n  <2>1. TAKE x \\\\in T, y \\\\in T\\n  <2>2. ASSUME x # y\\n         PROVE  g[x] # g[y]\\n    BY <1>1\\n<1>4. \\\\A y \\\\in S : \\\\E x \\\\in T : g[x] = y\\n  <2>1. TAKE y \\\\in S\\n  <2>2. f[y] \\\\in T\\n    BY DEF IsBijection\\n  <2>3. /\\\\ g[f[y]] \\\\in S\\n         /\\\\ f[g[f[y]]] = f[y]\\n    BY <1>1, <2>2\\n  <2>4. g[f[y]] = y\\n    BY <2>3 DEF IsBijection\\n  <2>. QED\\n    BY <2>2, <2>4\\n<1>5. IsBijection(g, T, S)\\n  BY <1>2, <1>3, <1>4 DEF IsBijection\\n<1>. QED\\n  BY <1>5\\n'''
p.write_text(s[:start]+new+s[end:])
PY
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps Sets_IsBijectionInverse.tla"

    69^I$

    73^I  ASSUME NEW f, NEW S, NEW T, $
    74^I         IsBijection(f, S, T) $
    75^I  PROVE  \E g : IsBijection(g, T, S)$

    85^I  <2>. QED$

    92^I         PROVE  g[x] # g[y]$

    94^I<1>4. \A y \in S : \E x \in T : g[x] = y$
    95^I  <2>1. TAKE y \in S$
    96^I  <2>2. f[y] \in T$
    97^I    BY DEF IsBijection$
    98^I  <2>3. /\ g[f[y]] \in S$

/bin/zsh -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
s=Path('Sets_IsBijectionInverse.tla').read_text()
start=s.index('THEOREM IsBijectionInverse ==')
prefix=s[:start]
mod=prefix + '''THEOREM IsBijectionInverse ==\\n  ASSUME NEW f, NEW S, NEW T, \\n         IsBijection(f, S, T) \\n  PROVE  TRUE\\n<1>. DEFINE g == [t \\\\in T |-> CHOOSE s \\\\in S : f[s] = t]\\n<1>. QED OBVIOUS\\n\\n=============================================================================\\n'''
Path('/tmp/CopyTest.tla').write_text(mod.replace('MODULE Sets_IsBijectionInverse','MODULE CopyTest'))
PY
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps /tmp/CopyTest.tla || true
nl -ba /tmp/CopyTest.tla | tail -30"

QED OBVIOUS\\n\\n=============================================================================\\n'''
Path('/tmp/CopyTest.tla').write_text(mod.replace('MODULE Sets_IsBijectionInverse','MODULE CopyTest'))
PY

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "cp Sets_IsBijectionInverse.tla /tmp/FullProofTest.tla
sed -i 's/MODULE Sets_IsBijectionInverse/MODULE FullProofTest/' /tmp/FullProofTest.tla
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps /tmp/FullProofTest.tla || true
# now make target TRUE but keep all steps after to see parser
python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('/tmp/FullProofTest.tla')
s=p.read_text()
s=s.replace('PROVE  \\\\E g : IsBijection(g, T, S)', 'PROVE  TRUE')
p.write_text(s)
PY
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps /tmp/FullProofTest.tla || true"

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
s=Path('Sets_IsBijectionInverse.tla').read_text()
start=s.index('THEOREM IsBijectionInverse ==')
end=s.index('\\n=============================================================================', start)
proof=s[start:end]
Path('/tmp/OnlyProof.txt').write_text(proof)
# create copy with partial variants: first nested theorem only
base=s[:start].replace('MODULE Sets_IsBijectionInverse','MODULE CopyPart')
variant='''THEOREM IsBijectionInverse ==\\n  ASSUME NEW f, NEW S, NEW T, \\n         IsBijection(f, S, T) \\n  PROVE  TRUE\\n<1>. DEFINE g == [t \\\\in T |-> CHOOSE s \\\\in S : f[s] = t]\\n<1>1. \\\\A t \\\\in T : /\\\\ g[t] \\\\in S\\n                      /\\\\ f[g[t]] = t\\n  <2>1. TAKE t \\\\in T\\n  <2>2. \\\\E s \\\\in S : f[s] = t\\n    BY DEF IsBijection\\n  <2>3. /\\\\ (CHOOSE s \\\\in S : f[s] = t) \\\\in S\\n         /\\\\ f[(CHOOSE s \\\\in S : f[s] = t)] = t\\n    BY <2>2\\n  <2>. QED\\n    BY <2>3 DEF g\\n<1>. QED OBVIOUS\\n\\n=============================================================================\\n'''
Path('/tmp/CopyPart.tla').write_text(base+variant)
PY
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps /tmp/CopyPart.tla || true
nl -ba /tmp/CopyPart.tla | tail -30"

from pathlib import Path

body='''THEOREM IsBijectionInverse ==\\n  ASSUME NEW f, NEW S, NEW T, \\n         IsBijection(f, S, T) \\n  PROVE  TRUE\\n<1>. DEFINE g == [t \\\\in T |-> CHOOSE s \\\\in S : f[s] = t]\\n<1>1. \\\\A t \\\\in T : /\\\\ g[t] \\\\in S\\n                      /\\\\ f[g[t]] = t\\n  <2>1. TAKE t \\\\in T\\n  <2>2. \\\\E s \\\\in S : f[s] = t\\n    BY DEF IsBijection\\n  <2>3. /\\\\ (CHOOSE s \\\\in S : f[s] = t) \\\\in S\\n         /\\\\ f[(CHOOSE s \\\\in S : f[s] = t)] = t\\n    BY <2>2\\n  <2>. QED\\n    BY <2>3 DEF g\\n<1>2. g \\\\in [T -> S]\\n  BY <1>1 DEF g\\n<1>3. \\\\A x, y \\\\in T : (x # y) => (g[x] # g[y])\\n  <2>1. TAKE x \\\\in T, y \\\\in T\\n  <2>2. ASSUME x # y\\n         PROVE  g[x] # g[y]\\n    BY <1>1\\n<1>. QED OBVIOUS\\n\\n=============================================================================\\n'''
Path('/tmp/CopyPart2.tla').write_text(prefix+body)
PY
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps /tmp/CopyPart2.tla || true"
